channel.title: healthTest
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from healthTest, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Merck and Mayo Clinic launch AI-driven precision medicine lab
item.description: 1. Merck and Mayo Clinic have launched a strategic collaboration to integrate multimodal clinical data with AI to accelerate drug discovery and target...

item.title: Telehealth abortion is in the courts. Share your experience.
item.description: Mifepristone is facing another major legal challenge.

item.title: Why supporting a shelter for women is now 'kind of radioactive'
item.description: That's how researcher Beatriz Garcia Nice describes the new U.S. stance under the Trump administration to programs addressing gender-based violence.

item.title: Lifestyle factors including higher body mass index and smoking are associated with changes in left-atrial size and function
item.description: 1. In this prospective cohort study, lifestyle behaviours, especially higher body mass index (BMI) and smoking, are associated with measurable changes...

item.title: Health informatics class gets an AI makeover to better prepare nursing students
item.description: From navigating electronic health records and HIPAA compliance to applying predictive analytics, health informatics is now essential to the daily work...

item.title: Does HRT Lower Osteoporosis Risk?
item.description: New research suggests early initiation of hormone replacement therapy could protect bone health. Medscape Medical News

item.title: The service of humanity: Recommitting to physicians’ ethical duties
item.description: Recently, my husband and I passed through Eureka, IL (pop. 5,200). We stopped at Eureka College, which is President Ronald Reagan’s alma mater and the...

item.title: Rebuilding patient trust through the evolutionary mismatch framework
item.description: How one reframing changed the way my patients think about me, and their health. Every day, a patient sits across from me, arms crossed, waiting for me...

item.title: Q&A: How the Dobbs decision and abortion restrictions have changed where medical students apply to residency programs
item.description: In the three-and-a-half years since the U.S. Supreme Court overturned the constitutional right to an abortion in Dobbs v. Jackson Women's Health Organ...

item.title: Systemic failure in professional environments: the myth of protection
item.description: There are moments when professional boundaries collapse under the weight of something more fundamental: our shared humanity. You do not have to be a p...

item.title: Early Neonatal Infection Linked to Cognitive Risk Later
item.description: Early-onset neonatal infection is linked to higher risks for intellectual disability and special educational needs during childhood, with meningitis c...

item.title: BMA Challenges Scotland’s Consultant Vacancy Figures
item.description: Doctors say FOI data reveal far more vacant consultant posts in NHS Scotland than official figures suggest, raising concerns over waiting lists and pa...

item.title: Online doctors popular but price-sensitive for young people, study finds
item.description: Young adults are more likely to consume health care when it is available via health care apps, and even more so if they do not have to pay patient fee...

item.title: Researchers discover hidden brain map that may improve epilepsy care
item.description: Mayo Clinic researchers have identified a hidden "movement map" deep within the brain—a discovery that could help surgeons reduce side effects from ep...

item.title: Common anticholinergic medicines may raise cardiovascular risk, large study suggests
item.description: People who use drugs with anticholinergic effects, including certain antidepressants, drugs for urinary incontinence and common antihistamines, are at...

item.title: Why I left the surgical-trauma ICU: a nurse’s story of burnout
item.description: After working close to 20 years in this medical-surgical ICU, I thought I was an expert. Infallible. I could do it all. I left my ICU comfort zone, my...

item.title: American health care policy reform: Why we need a bipartisan commission
item.description: America is ready for an honest conversation about health care policy before more people suffer and we go broke waiting for it to happen. Fully one-thi...

item.title: Is Vaginal Estrogen Safe After Endometrial Cancer?
item.description: Women who use vaginal estrogen to manage early menopause after treatment for endometrial cancer may not have an increased risk for recurrence. Medscap...

item.title: Patients and GPs Unite Over NHS ‘Maze’
item.description: GPs are spending up to a third of their time on paperwork, as patients and doctors warn that NHS bureaucracy is undermining care. Medscape News UK

item.title: Safety and efficacy profile of first-in-class oral p53 reactivator rezatapopt
item.description: 1. In this phase 1-2 single-group trial, the p53 reactivator rezatapopt was well-tolerated in adults with solid tumors harboring the TP53 Y220C mutati...

item.title: A new one-a-day-pill holds promise for HIV's 'forgotten population'
item.description: It's designed to take the place of complicated, multiple drug regimens that many people with HIV need to follow. And it's also beneficial because the ...

item.title: Smartphone-based digital ruler achieved high diagnostic performance for strabismus screening
item.description: 1. Wang and colleagues developed a system that uses deep learning algorithms to analyze cover tests recorded by suspected strabismus patients. 2. The ...

item.title: Ex vivo gene therapy for cystinosis had acceptable safety and improved disease biomarkers
item.description: 1. In this phase 1–2 open-label study, autologous lentiviral hematopoietic stem-cell gene therapy for cystinosis was associated with sustained engraft...

item.title: As more people turn to chatbots for health advice, studies say they may be led astray
item.description: People are turning to AI chatbots to help them with medical advice. Recent studies suggest these bots are not always helpful in making decisions about...

item.title: A homeless shelter and rehab center holds a marathon to get people back on their feet
item.description: Outside of Chicago, a homeless shelter and rehab center called Wayside Cross hosts a unique program to help people get back on their feet, one mile at...
